name: Al Udeid Air Base
hardening: Hardened aircraft shelters and protective revetments
description: Largest US military installation in the Middle East, hosting the USCENTCOM Forward Headquarters and the Combined Air Operations Center that directs all coalition air operations across the CENTCOM AOR. Operates over 90 combat and support aircraft generating approximately 14,700 sorties annually.

name: Camp Arifjan
equipment: Army Prepositioned Stock 5 (APS-5) — M1A2 Abrams, M2A3 Bradley, MRAPs
description: Primary US Army forward logistics and sustainment hub in the Middle East. Forward headquarters for U.S. Army Central (ARCENT). Manages Army Prepositioned Stock 5 with over 1.4 million annual equipment movements.

name: Ali Al Salem Air Base
hardening: Hardened aircraft shelters constructed post-2018 expansion
description: Joint US-Kuwaiti installation 37 km from the Iraqi border serving as the primary aerial port of debarkation for all US personnel transiting the CENTCOM theater. Kuwait Air Force conducts flight training and operates 28 Eurofighter Typhoons.

name: Camp Buehring
description: Primary US forward staging and pre-deployment training base in northwestern Kuwait. Accommodates up to 14,000 soldiers with live-fire ranges. Trains approximately 10,000 troops annually for deployment across the CENTCOM theater.

name: Naval Support Activity Bahrain
description: Headquarters of U.S. Naval Forces Central Command, the U.S. Fifth Fleet, and Combined Maritime Forces — the world's largest multinational naval partnership (47 nations). Supports 93 tenant commands overseeing maritime security across 2.5 million square miles.

name: Sheikh Isa Air Base
units: Royal Bahraini Air Force 1st Fighter Wing
description: Primary Royal Bahraini Air Force installation hosting the RBAF 1st Fighter Wing with F-16C/D fighters. Supports rotational US deployments with US-funded infrastructure improvements exceeding $45 million.

name: Al Dhafra Air Base
hardening: Hardened aircraft shelters for UAE and coalition aircraft
description: Tri-nation air base south of Abu Dhabi hosting USAF 380th AEW, UAE Western Air Command, and French Air Force. US contingent operates high-value ISR platforms including U-2S and RQ-4 Global Hawk, plus fifth-generation fighters (F-22A, F-35A).

name: Camp Lemonnier
units: Combined Joint Task Force — Horn of Africa (CJTF-HOA)
description: Largest US military installation in Africa. Home base for CJTF-HOA under AFRICOM. Supports approximately 4,500 personnel conducting counter-terrorism, ISR, and special operations across East Africa and the western Indian Ocean.

name: Incirlik Air Base
units: 10th Main Jet Base Command (Turkish Air Force)
equipment: B61 nuclear gravity bombs (~50, NATO nuclear sharing)
hardening: 57 hardened aircraft shelters; NATO's largest nuclear weapons storage (21 vaults)
description: Joint USAF-Turkish Air Force base near Adana. Hosts 57 hardened aircraft shelters including NATO's largest nuclear weapons storage site with an estimated 50 B61 gravity bombs. Provides critical aerial refueling, power projection, and nuclear deterrence.

name: Prince Sultan Air Base
units: Royal Saudi Air Force Wing 6 (E-3A AWACS)
description: Joint RSAF-USAF installation at Al Kharj, reactivated for US forces in 2019 with the 378th AEW. Provides integrated air and missile defense including Patriot and THAAD batteries. Two 4,000-meter runways among the longest in the region.

name: Muwaffaq Salti Air Base
hardening: Hardened aircraft shelters and reinforced concrete aprons
description: Royal Jordanian Air Force base near Azraq hosting significant US expeditionary air power. A $143 million US expansion added ISR, CAS, SOF, and airlift aprons. Key coalition staging point for Levant operations and forward air-defense node with THAAD and Patriot.

name: Al Tanf Garrison
description: Small US special-operations garrison on the M2 Baghdad-Damascus highway near the Syria-Iraq-Jordan tri-border. Anchored a 55-km deconfliction zone. US forces withdrew in February 2026.

name: Ain Al Asad Air Base
description: One of Iraq's largest airbases in Anbar province with three runways and extensive hardened infrastructure. Struck by Iranian ballistic missiles in January 2020. US forces completed withdrawal in January 2026.

name: Erbil Air Base
hardening: Concrete T-walls and hardened command facilities
description: Coalition air base co-located with Erbil International Airport in Kurdistan Region. Northern hub for CJTF-OIR advisory operations with Role 3 hospital. Repeatedly targeted by Iran-aligned militia drone and rocket attacks since 2020.

name: RAF Akrotiri
description: Britain's premier forward operating base in the Eastern Mediterranean, in the Western Sovereign Base Area of Cyprus. Typhoons based here struck Houthi targets in Yemen in 2024. Hosts a USAF U-2 detachment for high-altitude reconnaissance.

name: HMS Juffair
description: Royal Navy permanent support facility in the Persian Gulf, reopened 2018 at Mina Salman port, Manama. Supports UK Maritime Component Command and rotating warships for Gulf patrol and mine-clearance operations.

name: UK Naval Support Facility Duqm
units: UK Joint Logistics Support Base staff
description: Forward logistics and ship-repair hub at Duqm commercial port on Oman's Arabian Sea coast. Dry docks service every Royal Navy vessel including carriers and nuclear submarines. Strategically positioned outside the Strait of Hormuz.

name: French Naval Base Abu Dhabi
description: France's first permanent military base in the Persian Gulf, inaugurated 2009. Naval berth at Port Zayed accommodates all French warships except the carrier. Headquarters for French Indian Ocean naval operations.

name: Camp de la Paix (BA 188 Djibouti)
role: Power Projection into Africa and Indian Ocean
description: France's largest overseas military base, co-located with Djibouti-Ambouli International Airport. Hosts fighter, transport, and helicopter squadrons alongside Foreign Legion infantry. Primary staging base for French operations across East Africa, Red Sea, and Indian Ocean.
